title: 10 best herbal teas for white tongue

remedy_properties: Soothing and anti-inflammatory properties: Marshmallow root tea helps to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated mucous membranes, which can ease symptoms of white tongue such as discomfort and irritation.

Mucilages and demulcent properties: The thick, gel-like substance in marshmallow root helps to protect and coat the tongue and surrounding tissues, providing a barrier against irritants and soothing dryness and inflammation.

Antimicrobial and antibacterial properties: Marshmallow root tea has been shown to have antimicrobial and antibacterial properties, which can help combat infection and promote healing of the tongue and mouth, reducing symptoms of white tongue such as pain and discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Althaea officinalis, also known as marshmallow, teas helps with white tongue because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The root of the plant contains mucilages, which coat and calm the mucous membranes in the mouth, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. This can help to eliminate the bacteria and fungi that cause white tongue, a condition often associated with poor oral hygiene and an imbalance of the oral microbiome. Regular consumption of Althaea officinalis tea may also help to maintain a healthy oral environment.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: Mucilages help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the mouth, reducing inflammation and promoting the healing of white patches.

Triterpenoid Saponins (Specifically, Althaea officinalis contains many saponins, but no specific name is listed in the literature): These saponins have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce the inflammation associated with white tongue, promoting a healthy oral environment.

Phenolic Acids (Specifically, Althaea officinalis contains phenolic acids like Ferulic acid): Phenolic acids exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help protect the oral mucosa from oxidative stress and promote the resolution of white tongue symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are commonly used due to their rich content of mucilages, which provide soothing and protective properties to the mucous membranes.
Leaves: The leaves are also used, as they contain similar mucilages, which help to calm and heal the inflammation associated with white tongue.
Barks: The barks of Althaea officinalis are occasionally used, although less frequently than the roots and leaves, for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 to 2 ounces of dried root of the medicinal plant in late summer or early fall.
Rinse the dried root in cold water to remove any impurities or debris present on the surface.
Use a grinder or mortar to crush the dried root into a fine powder with a texture of flour.
Steep 1 teaspoon of the powdered root in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5 to 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any remaining particles or sediment.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 calendula officinalis flowers in the morning when they are dry and free of moisture.
Rinse the flowers gently with cold water to remove any dirt or debris from the surface.

Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the flower petals.

Combine 1 tablespoon of dried echinacea flowers and leaves with 1 quart of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the herbs to infuse and release their properties.
Strain the tea and serve immediately to enjoy the echinacea purpurea's health benefits.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to white tongue that are treated with herbal teas are various oral health issues. For instance, thrush, which causes a white or yellowish patch on the tongue and inner cheeks, can be treated with tea made from herbs like slippery elm, licorice root, and calendula. Herbal teas containing sage, chamomile, and peppermint have also been used to soothe mouth sores, gum inflammation, and bad breath.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for white tongue are a natural remedy that helps alleviate the symptoms of a white tongue, also known as oral candidiasis. This condition occurs when an overgrowth of yeast on the tongue causes a thick, white coating. Herbal teas such as peppermint, chamomile, and sage help soothe and balance the oral flora, reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy tongue. By incorporating these teas into one's daily routine, individuals can improve their overall oral health and boost their confidence, allowing them to live a more comfortable and active life.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal teas that help with white tongue is a blend of peppermint, sage, and echinacea. Peppermint tea helps to freshen breath and reduce inflammation, while sage tea has antibacterial properties that combat oral bacteria causing white tongue. Echinacea tea, rich in antioxidants, supports immune function and fights off infections. By drinking a combination of these teas, you can promote oral health, reduce white tongue symptoms, and maintain a healthy oral microbiome. Regular consumption may also prevent future occurrences.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Journal of Education and Health Promotion, herbal teas have shown potential in improving oral health. The study involved a systematic review of 49 original research studies and found that herbal products, including herbal teas, were effective and safe alternatives for oral health care. Specifically, herbal teas may be beneficial for conditions such as white tongue by reducing inflammation and promoting healthy oral flora, although this benefit is not explicitly stated in the provided study, it may be implied through the study's general findings on the effectiveness of herbal products in treating various oral diseases.
